def: s.m. Change; exchange; lieu, stead; substitute; return, recompense; compensation, reparation, restitution, indemnification, redress; requital, retaliation, reprisal, retribution, revenge

subs: (-ko), To make a return, give something in exchange or return, give an equivalent; to recompense, requite; to compensate; to make restitution, make amends, to indemnify
(-kā, for; -se, from), To take revenge; to retaliate; to wreak vengeance (on)
postp. In exchange (for -ke), in return (for), in lieu (of).

def: s.m. = badlā, q.v. s.m. Gold or silver cloth, brocade of silken stuff variegated ( = lappā); gold or silver thread.
more: n. m. See badlī . 1. A return; requital; remuneration ( mehntānah ); consideration; compensation ( evaz ); recompense ( ujrat ); equivalent; fee; reward; honorarium. 2. Indemnification; indemnity; reparation; restitution. 3. Reprisal; retaliation; revenge; retribution; atonement; redress; satisfaction; evil consequences. Gujar karī maiṅ missī khā-ke, rahī tarastī ghī se, Kisī janam kā badlā linā, nās gaī! mujh dhī se . Play. Chaubolā . On dry bread I am forced to live, for butter cravings vain, Perish my mother who for past birth's ills her child hath slain! ☞ She curses her mother who has married her to a poor man, in revenge possibly for ills she may have suffered at the hands of her daughter in a former birth. badlā denā , v. a. 1. To give an equivalent; requite; compensate; recompense; reward; pay for; make restitution. 2. To indemnify; make amends; make good. Gāt bajāt pī chale saj-…
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بدل (badal), from Arabic بَدَل (badal).

def: adj. 1. Hard-mouthed (a horse); not obedient to the rein. 2. Rude; disrespectful, or impudent (in speech). Zabāṅ saṅbhālo, ye muṅh-zoryāṅ garīboṅ par, Khudā kī sūṅ, koī tum sā bhī bad-lagām nahīṅ . Surūr.
s.f. Barter; exchange; price of exchange; something given in exchange ( = badalwāʼī).
more: n. f. Something given in exchange; barter; exchange. Ṭhaṭere ṭhaṭere badlāī hai! Prov. A brazier bargain with a brazier! (Diamond cut diamond).

def: v.t. To cause to change, &c. = badalwānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to be exchanged; to have, or get, changed or altered; to change, &c. (see badalnā).
more: v. a. To cause to alter, to change.

def: postp. See s.v. badlā. s.m. Gold or silver cloth, brocade of silken stuff variegated ( = lappā); gold or silver thread.

s.f. Cloudiness; a small bit of cloud

def: s.f. Change; exchange; barter; transfer; substitution, stead, lieu; person or thing taken in exchange for another; substitute; relief (of a watch)
more: n. f. 1. Change; exchange; barter; a person or thing taken in exchange for some other person or thing; locum tenens; relief (of a guard). Unkī badlī ho gaī . 2. Substitution; stead; lieu; room; place. badlī kar'nā , v. a. 1. To change; make an exchange or transfer. 2. To relieve one; take one's turn. badlī meṅ, badle meṅ , adv. In exchange; in return for; in place of; instead; in lieu of; mutato nomine ; in the room of. badlī meṅ honā , v. n. To take or supply the place of; to stand in the place of; officiate; act; to be put in, or to serve as a substitute; to do duty for.
(dimin. of بادل q. v.) s. f. 1. Cloudiness. 2. A small bit of cloud.
s. f. (dakh.) A small (leathern) bottle ( Harris ).
subs: (-kī), To change; to make an exchange; to transfer; to relieve (from duty or watch)
(-kī), To be in the room (of); to stand in the place (of); to officiate (for), do duty, or act as a substitute (for).

def: adj. 1. Unmannerly; disrespectful; impudent; impertinent. 2. Immodest; indecent; shameless ( besharm ).
adj. 1. Drunken; intoxicated. 2. Lustful; lewd; libidinous; lascivious.
n. f. 1. Drunkenness; inebriety; intoxication. 2. Lust; lewdness; lasciviousness.

adj. 1. Having a bad taste; unsavoury; ill-flavoured; insipid; tasteless. Āj kī dāl baṛī bad-mazah pakkī . 2. Ill; sick; out of sorts. Jī bad-mazah hai . 3. Angry; displeased; offended. Tum zarā sī bāt meṅ bad-mazah ho jāte ho .

def: n. f. Bad livelihood; vagrancy; loose conduct; villany.
v.n. To speak, discourse; to dispute, argue, wrangle, &c. ( = bād karnā, q.v.s.v. bād); to play on an instrument; to sound, strike (as a clock).

v.t. To speak: to settle, appoint, ordain; to take or invoke (as a witness); to predestinate, predestine; to stipulate, agree; to vow, pledge (one's) word; to wager, bet, stake; to acknowledge, own, admit; to observe or keep (a vow, &c.); to heed, mind, regard, obey; to empannel (a jury).
more: v. a. S. वदन from वद् to speak. 1. To predestinate; predestine; preordain; doom. Hogā wohī jo qismat meṅ badā hai . Prov. Saying. Whate'er your destiny assuredly will be. 2. To appoint; nominate; enrol. Gawāh badnā . 3. To wager; lay a bet; stake. Shart badnā . 4. To vow; swear; pledge one's word; promise. Badan badī thī raṅg-mahal kī, ṭūṭī maṅḍhaīyā meṅ lāë utāro, Badan badī thī lāl palaṅg kī, ṭūṭī kaṭhulyā pe lāë biṭhāyo . Wom. Song. You swore a splendid house I'd have, a hovel you have brought me to, You swore a rich bed should be mine, and lo! this rickety bed, fico! 5. To own; acknowledge; admit. Bāṅh chhuṭāë jāt ho, nibal jān-ke moë, Hirde meṅ se jāoge, to mard badūṅgī toë . Dohā. You shake me oft and go away, for me you weak esteem: Expel your image from my mind, then man you may beseem. 6. To heed; care for; regard; count ( ginnā ). Woh mujhe kuchh nahīṅ badtā . Wom.
v. a. 1. To wager, to bet. 2. To settle, to predestinate, to take (as witness), to obey, to acknowledge, to agree, not to refuse.

Ill-report; defamation; disrepute; dishonor; slur; stigma ( dhabbā ).

Every man's house is his castle. 3. One skilled in any art (ustād, 2) . Apne kām kā bādshāh hai. bādshāh-zādā, shah-zāda , n. m. A king's son; prince. bādshāh-zādī , n. f. Compare Maithili बादसाह (bādᵊsāh) / 𑒥𑒰𑒠𑒮𑒰𑒯 (bādasāha), Punjabi ਬਾਦਸ਼ਾਹ (bādśāh) / بادْشاہ (bādśāh), Sindhi بادشاهُ (bādśāhu) / बादशाहु, Gujarati બાદશાહ (bādśāh), Marathi बादशहा (bādaśhā), Bengali বাদশাহ (badośah), Telugu పాదుషా (pāduṣā).

def: n. f. 1. Kingdom; sovereignty; monarchy. 2. State; government; rule. 3. Realm; empire; dominion; sceptre. bādshāhat karnā, rāj karnā , v. a. To hold the sceptre; reign; govern; rule.

s.m. Presents of sweetmeats, &c., forming part of an offering to a deity, or prepared on festive occasions, such as marriages, &c., and sent to friends and acquaintances; — share, allotted portion.

= bāṅ-bāṅ, q.v. s.f. Prating, jabber, senseless talk

n. f. S. वल्गा from वला to go. A rein; bridle ( lagām ). bāg-pakṛā'ī , n. f. 1. Holding the horse of the bride-groom by the dhiyanās or sons-in-law of his family, being a part of the Hindu marriage ceremony. 2. The reward paid to the above-named honorary grooms.

s. f. A rein, a bridle. باگ لينا bāg lenā, To take the rein; to curb or pull up (a horse, &c.). باگ موڙنا bāg moṛnā, To turn the reins: and, this phrase is applied to the drying up of the small-pox; thus, when the pustules begin to blacken and dry up, they say, سيتلاني باگ موڙي sītlā ne bāg moṛī, or ماتاني باگ موڙي mātā ne bāg moṛī. باگ هاتهـ سي چهوٿنا bāg hāth se chhūṭnā, The reins to have slipped from the hand, denotes the loss of choice, power or controul.
subs: s.f. Tiger and goat; lion and kid; — a boy's game (similar to our 'fox and geese')
bāg-nak, s.m. Tiger's claw (set in silver and worn as a charm); a weapon made of steel, resembling huge claws, and fitting upon the hands (cf. baghnā and bag-nahā).

def: s.f. Rein; bridle ( = lagām)

s.f. Holding the horse of a bridegroom by the sons-in-law of his family (part of a Hindū marriage ceremony); — the reward or present given to those who hold the bridegroom's horse
s.f. A long rope with which horses are led; halter
To give a loose rein (literally and figuratively); to give the rein, let (a horse) out, set (a horse) at full gallop; to leave one to himself
(-kī), To rein in (a horse, &c.); to curb, check, restrain, control
v.n. To turn the reins; turn away or back; to take a turn, to dry up (the pustules of small-pox)
(-kī), To lose control or power (over); to get beyond control; to lose a chance or opportunity
(-kī), To let the rein loose, &c. = bāg ḍhīlī karnā, &c.

s.f. Slow, measured walk (like a crane's)
(S. vaka+rājā), s.m. King of the cranes

Rus. n. f. 1. See ārāish (2). 2. ( Met ). Children; offspring; family; posterity. bāg bāg honā , v. n. To rejoice or exult greatly; leap with joy.
n. m. bā/?/gīchī , n. f. A small garden in which flowers, fruits, or vegetables are grown ( bāṛī ).
more: (dimin. of باغ) s. m. A small garden, a private garden.

def: n. m. H. bi excess, gāchhī tree. A small garden.
s.m. Suit of clothes, dress, vestment; robe of honour ( = ḵẖilʻat, q.v.); wedding garment (bridegroom's).
more: n. m. S. वस्त्र cloth. 1. A dress of honor; a khilat . 2. A wedding garment; the dress worn by the bridegroom. 1. Aṅg sohe kesaryā bāgā, gale bich mutyan kī mālā . Marriage Song. The crimson dress the body adorns, the string of pearls the neck becomes. 2. Woh rākh malī jo mukh, tan par, woh rākh na thī, woh thā ubṭan, Aur lāl shahānā bāgā thā, woh gerwā-raṅgā pairāhan. (Mahādeo kā byāh) . Nazīr. His face and body, not with ashes grimed, But with cosmetic rich and rare sublimed, And so the yellow earth his garment seemed In truth shone as the bridegroom's raiment red. 3. A suit; apparel. Kai bāge dīye?
s. m. A vestment; honorary dress.

def: v.n. To return; to retreat; to go astray; to be spoiled, &c. ( = bigaṛnā).
more: Rus. v. n. S. वि back, गम् go. To return; come back ( lauṭnā ). Bole dādar, mor, papīhā, koël bolī, Kad bagdeṅge piu, bilamh se phaṭ gaī cholī . Song. Birah. I hear the frog, the peacock, sparrow-hawk and cuckoo too, When will my love return, delay my bodice bursts anew.
v. n. To return: to be spoiled.

def: adv. In time, opportunely, seasonably.
prep. Without, exclusive (of, -ke), excluding; except, besides, independent (of)
more: P. bidūn; H. binā , adv. Without; exclusive of; excluding; omitting; barring; except; save and except; save; beside; but; independent of.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic بِغَيْرِ (bi-ḡayri).

def: s.f. Side, flank; armpit; embrace; gusset in the armpit of a garment; — adv. On one side, aside, out of the way; by the side (of), close by
more: n. f. See ارَس پرَوس 1. The armpit; side. Muṅh meṅ Shekh Farīd, bagal meṅ īṅṭeṅ . Prov. In speech Shekh Farid (a saint), and bricks under his arm. 2. The piece, or gusset under the armpit. bagal , adv. On one side; aside; hard by. bagal-bilāī , P. H. n. f. A painful glandular swelling in the armpit, so called because the old wives of India believe that it is cured by getting a cat to lick it ( kakrālī ). bagal jānā, bagal honā, baglā'nā , v. n. To get out of the way; turn aside; make way. bagal kā dushman, baglī ghūṅsā , n. m. A traitor; a secret enemy. Ise dost na jāno, ye baglī ghūṅsā hai . Take him not for a friend, he'll smite you under your arm. bagal garm karnā , v. n. Slang. To embrace, or sleep with (a woman). bagal-gandh , n. f. Stinking armpits. bagal-gīr honā , v. n. To embrace by way of salutation; clasp; hug. Haiṅ woh rūṭhe huë, āne ke nahīṅ āj, Īd kā roz hai, jā ho-ke bagal…

s. f. 1. The armpit. 2. Embrace. بغل بجانا bag̠ẖal bajānā, To be highly pleased, to be in triumph, to jeer, jest, chuckle. بغل پرورده bag̠ẖal parwarda, Brought up in the arms; a darling or favourite child. بغل جاناياهوجانا bag̠ẖal jānā yā hojānā, To go out of the way. بغل زن bag̠ẖal-zan, Insulting, provoking, rejoicing at the misfortune of another. بغل سونگنا bag̠ẖal sūṅgnā, To repent.بغل گرم کرنا bag̠ẖal garm karnā, To embrace a woman. بغل گنده bag̠ẖal gandh, Stinking armpits. بغل گير bag̠ẖal gīr, An embracer in the Hindū mode. بغل گيرهونا bag̠ẖal-gīr honā, To embrace. بغل گيري bag̠ẖal-gīr-ī, s. f. Embracing. بغل گيري کرني bag̠ẖal-gīr-ī karnī, To embrace. بغل مين دبانا bag̠ẖal meṅ dabānā, To get possession of anotherʼs goods by fraud. بغل مين لينا bag̠ẖal meṅ lenā, To take in oneʼs arms, to embrace. بغل مين مارنا bag̠ẖal meṅ mārnā, To conceal under oneʼs arms. بغل مين منڐي ڐالنا bag̠ẖal…
subs: s.f. A painful glandular swelling in the armpit (so called because it is believed, by the old wives of India, to be most effectually relieved and dispersed by getting a cat to lick it; — syn. kakrālī)
s.m. A kind of robe tied under the armpits; rope tied under the armpits
adj. & s.m. Brought up in the arms; — a darling or favourite child
bag̠al hona or ho-jānā, v.n. To go aside, get out of the way, make way
v.n. lit. 'To smell the arm-pits'; to be abashed or ashamed, be penitent, to repent

adj. & s.m. Embracing; embracer (after the Oriental fashion); — bag̠al-gīr honā (-se), To embrace (by way of salutation); to clasp, hug; — bag̠al-gīrī, s.f. Embracing, clasping; — bag̠al-gīrī karnā, v.t. To embrace
v.t. To put on one side, put out of the way (a horse, carriage, &c.)
adv. At the side, by the side (of), close by
v.n. To put faith away or aside, to prove faithless or treacherous
v.t. To conceal (a thing) under the arm; to carry off (a thing) under the arm
v.t. To take into the arms, to embrace
bag̠al-meṅ muṅh ḍālnā, v.n. To hide the head or face, be ashamed or abashed

v.n. To produce a sound by placing the hands under the armpits and striking the arms against them; to flap the wings; to be highly pleased, be in great glee, be in triumph, to exult, chuckle, crow (over)
v.n. To look blank or foolish; to look from side to side in shame or discomfiture, be ashamed, to desire to escape or slink away.

def: W. Rus. n. m. 1. An enclosure; a house. O le, paṛosan, jhoṅpṛā, nit uṭh kartī rāṛ, Ādhā bagaṛ buhārtī, ab sārā hī buhār . Rus. Song. There take the whole hut to yourself, each morn you wake from sleep, A row with me you make, for half, the whole yard you can sweep. 2. Neighbourhood ( paṛos ). Yā kā ghar to mhāre bagaṛ meṅ saë . v.t. = baghārnā, q.v. v.t. To brown onions and spices in heated oil or ghī, as a relish or seasoning for meat, &c.; to season (baghārnā consists in heating oil or ghī, throwing in some onions and other seasonings, then stirring and rubbing the mixture rapidly till thoroughly browned, when the meat, &c. to be dressed is added, and the stirring continued).

v.n. To be expanded, be spread, &c. = bikharnā, q.v. v.n. To be scattered, strewn, spread, dispersed; to be sown broadcast; to be disordered, disarranged, tossed, dishevelled (as hair); to be wasted, spoiled, ruined; to change for the worse, become worse; to become angry or enraged
more: v. n. S. विघटन from विघट् to be destroyed. 1. To lose virtue, grace, beauty, etc. 2. To be spoilt, deteriorated, degenerated; become worse. 3. To fall off; retrograde; lose skill; grow rusty. Hāth bigaṛ gayā . 4. To become hoarse, husky, false. Gāte gāte bigaṛ gayā . 5. To become musty, sticky, rancid, etc. Rāt kā khānā bigaṛ gayā . 6. To moulder; rot; decay. Phr. To go on from bad to worse; go to the dogs; go to rack and ruin. 7. To be damaged, marred, injured. 8. To become worse; to be sinking or dying. Inkā hāl bigaṛ gayā, shāyad hī bacheṅ . 9. To be distorted, disfigured, defaced. Lage muṅh bhī chiṛāne, dete dete gālyāṅ, sāhib, Zabāṅ bigṛī to bigṛī thī, khabar līje, dahan bigṛā . From foul abuse you've taken to making faces now, Foul-mouthed you were, see now your face distorted grow. 10. To become useless, or unserviceable. 11. To be rubbed out, obliterated, effaced ( miṭnā ). Likkh…

def: v.t. To brown onions and spices in heated oil or ghī, as a relish or seasoning for meat, &c.; to season (baghārnā consists in heating oil or ghī, throwing in some onions and other seasonings, then stirring and rubbing the mixture rapidly till thoroughly browned, when the meat, &c. to be dressed is added, and the stirring continued).
more: Rus. v. a. 1. To fry spices in butter ( chhauṅknā ). Chhīle chār, baghāre pāṅch! Mah. Wom. Prov. You peeled but four and brought out five! (Said tauntingly to her conceited daughter-in-law). 2. To boast; brag; vaunt; talk big. Zabāṅ hai jiske ishārat se woh pukāre hai, Jo gūṅgā hai woh khaṛā fārsī baghāre hai! The world. Nazīr. And men with tongues all speak by signs, While dumb ones spout grand Persian lines!

ety: S. वकोट; or S. वक+H. dim. aff. lā
def: s.m. Heron; crane, Ardea torra and putea (in Bengal usually called, by Europeans, 'the paddy-bird,' because of its being commonly found in rice fields)
more: Poet. n. m. S. वक, Palī. bako . A paddy-bird; heron; stork; Ardea torra and putea. Buch. Cucurbita melopepo . Wat. 1. Tan gorā, man sāṅvrā, bagle kā sā bhek, To se to kāgā bhalā, bāhar bhītar ek . Dohā. Fair body and black heart, you ever play the false stork's part, Better than you the crow, the same inside and out, both swart. ☞ The stork standing on one leg, seemingly unconcerned but intent on his prey, represents a pious ascetic with murder in his heart. 2. Baglā māre, pankh hāth . Prov. A paddy-bird slain, only feathers your gain. baglā-bhagat , n. m. A hypocrite ( kapṭī ); one who pretends or tries to pass for a bhagat ; a cunning, artful fellow ( makkār ). baglo'lo , n. m. 1. The young of a baglā , q. v. 2. A toy. bag-hans , n. m. The grey goose.
s. m. A crane or heron. (Ardea torra and putea. Buch. ). See بک bak. بگلابهگت baglā bhagat, A hypocrite.
subs: s.m. False devotee (one whose acts or observances are like those of a crane), hypocrite (syn. kapṭī), pretender; cunning, artful fellow (syn. makkār).

def: v.n. To go aside, move out of the way, make way ( = bag̠al ho-jānā).
more: v. To make way, to get out of the way.

def: adj. Relating to, or belonging to, the side, or the armpit; axillary; side, at the side, lateral; — s.f. A purse; a small bag for needles and thread (such being usually carried at the side; syn. tile-dānī); — a disorder in camels (when they rub the thigh against the belly); — a kind of dumb-bell used in exercise; a mode of playing with foils or fencing-sticks (ḍanḍās); — a trick in wrestling (slipping away from under the antagonist's arm); — the action of a bird hiding its head under its wing; a sort of dress, the bottom of which is under the armpits
more: adj. Belonging to the armpit, or side; lateral. baglī , n. f. 1. E. A small bag for needles and thread ( tile-dānī ). 2. A disorder in camels, rubbing the thigh against the belly. 3. A trick in wrestling. Baglī ḍūb-ke nikal gayā . 4. A mode of playing ḍanḍās (sticks). baglī denā , E. v. a. To break into a house by removing a portion of the wall and so unbolting the door.

(from بغل) 1. adj. Relating to the armpit, axillary. 2. s. f. A disorder in camels when they rub the thigh against the belly. 3. A purse. 4. A kind of dumb bell used in exercise. 5. A sort of dress, the bottom of which is under the armpits. 6. The action of a bird putting its head under its wing. بغلي تکيه bag̠ẖlī takiya, A bolster for the sides.
subs: s.m. Side-screw; — bag̠lī takiyā, s.m. Bolster for the sides, or at the sides
(-meṅ), To break into a house by removing a portion of the wall, and so unbolting the door
s.m. lit. 'Side-blow'; secret enemy, traitor ( = bag̠al-kā dushman).

def: adj. & adv. In the usual state, in the former state, as before, in statu quo; in a good state or condition, well, in good health; flourishing, prosperous, well-to-do; established, confirmed, maintained; re-established, reinstated, restored (to health or office); in office, in work or service
more: adj. 1. Established; upheld; confirmed; maintained ( bar-qarār ). 2. In a good state or condition; well; in good health ( tan-durust ). Shukr-i-Khudā ki āj tabīat ba-hāl hai . Sehr. 3. Re-instated; re-established; restored (to health or office); in office. 4. Flourishing; thriving; set up; well-to-do. bahāl rahnā , v. n. To remain intact; to be unimpaired. bahāl rakhnā , v. a. To maintain; uphold; confirm; continue. bahāl karnā , v. a. 1. To establish; confirm; affirm. 2. To re-instate; restore; restore to health; re-establish. 3. To put back (in its place); revive (a law). bahāl honā , v. n. 1. To be restored, re-instated. 2. To recover; to be restored to health.
In state or condition; adj. Unaltered, happy, flourishing, in office, reinstated, restored. بحال کرنا ba-ḥāl karnā, To reinstate, reestablish, replace, restore.
subs: To return to a former state or condition, to recover oneself, be restored, be oneself again; to be reinstated

v.t. To establish, confirm; to reinstate, replace, restore (to, -par, office), re-establish, revive (a statute, &c.); to restore to health

adj. & adv. According, harmonious, friendly; in accord, at one.

subs: baham pahuṅćnā, v.n. To be procured, acquired, or obtained; to come to hand
v.t. To convey, supply, provide, procure, acquire, get; to bring about, form (a friendship, &c.)

def: n. m. 1. Plea; pretext; pretence; sham. Tū merā, maiṅ terī, we lālā, diloṅ kī ghunḍī khol, Aī tere dekhan ko, tū kīsī bahāne bol . Song. Chhallā. You, Sir, are mine, and I am yours, your purse-strings open now, To you I come to show myself, a word to me but throw. 2. A blind; feint; ruse ; stratagem. 3. Excuse; a false or lame excuse. 4. Evasion; putting off; shuffling. 5. Means; cause. Hīle rizaq, bahāne maut . Prov. For food a means, for death a cause. bahā'nā-sāz , n. m. An impostor; one who shams. bahānā k . v. n. def: n. f. S. हरित Green. 1. Spring ( basant ); vernal equinox. 1. Harī ḍāl ke bhuṭṭe kī bahār! Delhī Street cry. The spring time of maize with fresh green leaves, ho! 2. Ek to thā hī divānā, tis par āī ye bahār! Prov. Mad he was, and lo, the Spring has come! 2. Fine scenery; a fine view, a picture or landscape. 3. Verdure; life; bloom; flourishing state. 4. The pleasures of sense, taste, or culture; exquisite enjoyment. 1. Shab ko chaman meṅ wāh! wāh! kyā hī, bahār thī machī, Phūl khile the phūl phūl, gunche khile kalī kalī, Belā, chamelī, rāë-bel, motyā, jūh/?/, sevtī, Bād-i-sabā bhī chaltī thī, itr o gulāb meṅ basī, Hauz paṛe chhalakte the, nahr hiloreṅ letī thī, Shokh bagal meṅ guncha-lab, maë kī nashoṅ kī tāzgī, Aish o tarab kī lahr meṅ rāt jab ādhī ḍhal gaī, Is meṅ kahīṅ se, haë gazab! niklī jo makr chāṅdnī, Subah ke ḍar se haṛ-baṛā yār ne ghar kī rāh lī, Ham bhī dagā meṅ ā gaë, muft bahār lut gaī . Ālam-i-bahār. Spring. Nazīr. Last night so beautiful the garden wantoned like the Spring, The flowers full-blown in ripened bloom, the young buds just opening, Roses and jasmines of all kinds, light winds in roses bathed, While fountains played, and streamlets coursed in graceful waves and curled, My saucy love my arms enclosed, a bud-like mouth, renewed Each time th'enlivening grape: when in the waves of pleasure steeped, All in the middle wane of night the glimmer false of morn Appeared, alas! In fear she hurried off, was homeward borne, I too deceived, my pleasure all away from me was torn! 2. Gol gāt, kāman nipaṭ, raṅg-ras bharā khilāṛ, Lākh * pate kī bāt kahī, mar-kar det bahār . Riddle. Ans. Qumqumā . q. v. * Lākh 1. One lac. 2. Shell lac. A rounded form most delicate, with luscious sport is here, A millionnaire is he, where'er he strikes spring's joys appear. 5. Zenith; climax; culminating point. Har dāṅt chane se daltā ho, tab dekh bahāreṅ jāṛe kī . Nazīr. When the teeth chatter like peas in millstones ground, then see great winter in excelsior. 6. ( Ironic .) Something sweet or fine; 'what's what.' Agar merā kām bigṛā to iskī bahār dikhāūṅgā. bahār par ānā , v. n. To blossom; bloom. bahār lūṭnā , v. n. To enjoy the height of pleasure; revel or riot in. bahār kā tel , n. m. Oil scented in orange flower.

def: adv. S. वहिर. 1. Externally; outward; outside; without; out; abroad; ab extra . Phr.—Out of doors; in the open air; sub jove . 1. Ghar khīr, to bāhar bhī khīr . Prov. Who dine at home on dainty food, when they dine out will get as good. 2. Bāhar lambī lambī dhotī, bhītar maṛwe kī roṭī . E. Prov. For going out an ample dhotī (fine clothes), at home the coarsest fare. 2. Independent or exclusive of; beyond; without. Ham kisī bāt se bāhar nahīṅ . I agree to every thing. 3. Abroad; on a journey or tour. Aglā to bahut kar-ke bāhar rahe hai . Hin. Wom. 4. Out of town; in the suburb or vicinity. Dillī kī beṭī, Muthrā kī gāë, Karam phūṭe to bāhar jäë . Prov. A Delhi lass, a Mathra cow, Sad fate, that they e're out should go! bāhar , intj. Out! away! get out. bāhar bhītar , adv. In and out; within and without. bāhar bhītar , n. m. 1. Inside and outside; ingress and egress. 2. Going out; visits; high days and holidays. 3. (Hin. Wom). A necessary; privy; latrine. bāhar bhūm , n. f. Land lying beyond the village boundaries. bāhar jānā , v. n. To go out; go on a journey. bāhar kar-ke , adv. Except; excepted; exclusive of; exempt. bā'har karnā, yā nikālnā , v. a. 1. To exclude; leave out; eliminate; strike out; omit. 2. To turn out; dismiss; expel; put out or away. 3. To oust; eject; dispossess. Khet se bāhar kar dīyā . 4. To put out of caste; excommunicate. 5. To repudiate; divorce (a wife). Dharam karo, bhaī, dharam karo, ghar kī jorū bāhar karo . Mendicant's cry. Now give your charity, good men, your charity, and turn out of the house your wives. ☞ It is still the custom for Rajas at great pilgrimages to make a gift of the wife, loaded with jewels, to the family priest from whom she is bought back again at such price as the parties may agree upon. bāhar kī bū, gaṅvār-pan , n. lit . A smell of the country. Rusticity; clownishness; boorishness. bāhar kī phirne-wālī, andar bāhar kī jāne-wālī , n. f. Women who go about in the streets, marketing, etc. generally with unveiled faces in contradistinction to the parde-wālī . q. v. bāhar le jānā , v. n. To export; take abroad. bāhar nikalnā , v. n. 1. To come out of; come outside; go about in public. 2. To withdraw from; wash one's hands of. bāhar nikalne ke, yā bar-āmad hone-ke waqt . While engaged in a procession. G. G. bāhar-wālā , n. m. bāhar-wālī , n. f. Hin. Wom. A sweeper. bāhar honā , v. n. 1. To be outside of, or not included in. 2. To deviate; diverge; wander from. 3. To dissent from; disagree in.
